perturbation.original_text: If either party elects not to pursue any further business und ertaking, each party will promptly return upon request all tangible informati on including any and all copies thereof relating to all Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: If either party elects not to pursue any further business und ertaking, each party will promptly return upon request all tangible informati on including any and all copies thereof relating to all Confidential Information. However, the receiving party is permitted to retain one archival copy of the Confidential Information for legal compliance purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text mandates the return of 'all' tangible information upon request if further business is not pursued. The addition, 'However, the receiving party is permitted to retain one archival copy of the Confidential Information for legal compliance purposes,' directly contradicts this absolute requirement. It creates uncertainty about whether 'all' really means 'all', specifically within Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either party elects not to pursue any further business und ertaking, each party will promptly return upon request all tangible informati on including any and all copies thereof relating to all Confidential Information. However, the receiving party is permitted to retain one archival copy of the Confidential Information for legal compliance purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states 'all' tangible information must be returned. The changed text adds an exception allowing the receiving party to retain one archival copy. This directly conflicts with the original mandate to return *all* information and creates an inconsistency, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter , including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter , including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights. However, if the parties collaborate and create new intellectual property jointly, then KERBER APPLIED RESEARCH INC. will own all such new intellectual property.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding ownership of intellectual property. The original text states that 'each party shall retain all rights of ownership.' The addition stating 'if the parties collaborate and create new intellectual property jointly, then KERBER APPLIED RESEARCH INC. will own all such new intellectual property' directly contradicts this by assigning complete ownership to KERBER in cases of joint creation. This creates ambiguity about ownership rights under collaboration, specifically within Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall retain all rights of ownership over all intellectual property associated with the above referenced subject matter , including the rights of ownership of patents, trademarks and copyrights. However, if the parties collaborate and create new intellectual property jointly, then KERBER APPLIED RESEARCH INC. will own all such new intellectual property.
perturbation.justification: The original text asserts that 'each party shall retain all rights of ownership'. The changed text adds a condition where KERBER APPLIED RESEARCH INC. will own all new intellectual property jointly, directly contradicting the initial statement by assigning complete ownership to KERBER in specific cases. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, and the Perturbation Explanation states it creates ambiguity about ownership rights under collaboration. This introduces a strong in-text contradiction, where one section of the document clearly conflicts with another.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario, except that any disputes relating to intellectual property shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a conflict of laws. The original text specifies that the entire agreement is governed by Ontario law. By adding 'except that any disputes relating to intellectual property shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware', the contract now contains conflicting clauses regarding which jurisdiction's laws apply, specifically within Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and interpreted in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario, except that any disputes relating to intellectual property shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the entire agreement is governed by Ontario law. The changed text adds an exception, stating that intellectual property disputes are governed by Delaware law. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, meaning that the change introduces conflicting clauses regarding jurisdiction, creating a direct in-text contradiction. The Perturbation Explanation explicitly states this conflict.

perturbation.original_text: The furnishing of Confidential Information pursu ant to this Agreement shall not be construed as granting or conferring, either expr essly or implicitly, any rights, licences or relationships.
perturbation.changed_text: The furnishing of Confidential Information pursu ant to this Agreement shall not be construed as granting or conferring, either expr essly or implicitly, any rights, licences or relationships. However, the receiving party shall be granted a non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal research purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that providing confidential information does not grant any rights or licenses. The added sentence, granting a non-exclusive license for internal research, directly contradicts this initial statement, specifically within Section 5. This creates an in-text contradiction by introducing ambiguity about the scope of rights conferred.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The furnishing of Confidential Information pursu ant to this Agreement shall not be construed as granting or conferring, either expr essly or implicitly, any rights, licences or relationships. However, the receiving party shall be granted a non-exclusive license to use the Confidential Information for internal research purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that furnishing confidential information does not grant any rights or licenses. The changed text adds a sentence granting a non-exclusive license for internal research. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating an in-text contradiction as it introduces a specific right (a license) that was previously denied.
